LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF W. LAROSE'S RENEWED MOTION FOR AN ORDER CERTIFYING A CLASS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED ANY ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTING CLASS TREATMENT. PLAINTIFF'S PAPERS FALL SHORT OF SATISFYING HER BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THIS CASE SHOULD BE CERTIFIED AS A CLASS ACTION. SHE HAS PROVIDED NO FURTHER EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT: 1) COMMON ISSUES OF LAW AND/OR FACT PREDOMINATE, 2) HER CLAIMS ARE TYPICAL OF THE CLAIMS OF OTHER CLASS MEMBERS, 3) CLASS CERTIFICATION IS THE BEST METHOD OF RESOLVING THIS CASE; AND 4) SHE HAS A FEASIBLE PLAN FOR THE MANAGEMENT AND RESOLUTION OF INDIVIDUAL ISSUES. AS SHOWN BY THE EVIDENCE ON THE EARLIER MOTION, THE SIGNIFICANT DIFFERENCES IN THE DUTIES AND CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE GENERAL MANAGERS FROM STORE TO STORE MAKES IT UNLIKELY THAT CLASS TREATMENT IS THE BEST METHOD FOR ADJUDICATING THE CLAIMS OF THE GENERAL MANAGERS. NOTHING IN THIS RENEWED MOTION CHANGES THE PRIOR DETERMINATION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: KELLEY DE YOUNG, CSR #6780 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF W. LAROSE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT ADDING TWO NEW PLAINTIFFS IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA HAS A LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF PERMITTING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS. IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON PLAINTIFF'S RENEWED MOTION FOR AN ORDER CERTIFYING A CLASS, THE TWO NEW PLAINTIFFS NEED TO PURSUE THEIR CLAIMS INDIVIDUALLY AND JUDICIAL ECONOMY IS SERVED BY THEIR DOING SO IN THIS CASE. ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT PERCEIVE ANY DEFICIENCIES IN THE CLAIMS OF THE NEW PLAINTIFFS, ANY ASSERTED DEFICIENCIES ARE BETTER ADDRESSED BY A PLEADING MOTION SUCH AS A DEMURRER OR MOTION TO STRIKE RATHER THAN DENYING PERMISSION TO THE NEW PLAINTIFFS TO BE ADDED TO THIS LAWSUIT. DEFENDANT WILLIAMS-SONOMA STORES, INC. HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT WILL BE PREJUDICED BY THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Renewed Motion For Order Certifying Class, Etc.
Off Calendar Nov-04-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff W. LaRose's renewed motion for an order certifying a class is denied. Plaintiff has not provided any additional evidence supporting class treatment. Plaintiff's papers fall short of satisfying her burden of showing that this case should be certified as a class action. She has provided no further evidence showing that: 1) common issues of law and/or fact predominate, 2) her claims are typical of the claims of other class members, 3) class certification is the best method of resolving this case; and 4) she has a feasible plan for the management and resolution of individual issues. As shown by the evidence on the earlier motion, the significant differences in the duties and circumstances of the general managers from store to store makes it unlikely that class treatment is the best method for adjudicating the claims of the general managers. Nothing in this renewed motion changes the prior determination. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kelley De Young, CSR #6780 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To File Proposed Fourth Amended Complaint Adding New Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Nov-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff W. LaRose's motion for leave to file a fourth amended complaint adding two new plaintiffs is granted. California has a liberal policy in favor of permitting amendments to pleadings. In light of the ruling on plaintiff's renewed motion for an order certifying a class, the two new plaintiffs need to pursue their claims individually and judicial economy is served by their doing so in this case. Although the court does not perceive any deficiencies in the claims of the new plaintiffs, any asserted deficiencies are better addressed by a pleading motion such as a demurrer or motion to strike rather than denying permission to the new plaintiffs to be added to this lawsuit. Defendant Williams-Sonoma Stores, Inc. has not shown that it will be prejudiced by the granting of this motion. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kelley De Young, CSR #6780 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Order Certifying Class, Etc.
Off Calendar Apr-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Gallo of Law Offices of David Gallo telephone number 619-988-8800 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff W. LaRose.
Jessica Perry and Julia Riechert of Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe, LLP telephone number 650-614-7350 for Defendant Williams-Sonoma, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed and modified its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF W LAROSE'S Motion For Order Certifying Class is denied without prejudice to her refiling a motion for class certification. Plaintiff's papers in opposition to defendant's motion to deny class certification and in support of her motion fall short of satisfying her burden of showing that this case should be certified as a class action. She has not shown that: 1) common issues of law and/or fact predominate, 2) her claims are typical of the claims of other class members, 3) class certification is the best method of resolving this case; and 4) she has a feasible plan for the management and resolution of individual issues. The court is particularly concerned that plaintiff apparently seeks to certify a class as to an "issue" rather than as to a claim or claims without a workable plan how to resolve the remaining individual issues. Because plaintiff wishes to take additional discovery and the court cannot now say that plaintiff would never be able to meet her burden to certify a class, plaintiff is permitted to refile her motion if in good faith she believes that she has sufficient evidence to support class treatment. In any refiled motion if plaintiff seeks class certification as to an issue or issues, rather than a claim or claims, it will be extremely important that she state with particularity her plan for resolving all issues for which she is not seeking class certification. The deadline for Plaintiff to file a motion for class certification is October 6, 2016. If said motion has not been filed by the set deadline, then Defendant's motion to deny class certification will be granted. Defendant may come in ex parte. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the Court's ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
